title: SIDPIN | Case Studies & Ecosystem (Extended)
prompt:
Add a new section titled 'Featured Case Studies' directly below the 'Why Choose SIDPIN' section and above the final CTA. 

Section Title: Featured Case Studies
Headline: Real Brands. Real Stories. Real Growth.
Subtext: Every project is more than content, design, or marketing. It's about creating meaningful business impact.

Use large Apple-inspired expandable cards for:
1. Panchbhoot Yog (Social Media & Brand Building) - Visuals: Instagram reels, growth analytics.
2. Rudra Dharma (Website & Social Media) - Visuals: Website mockups, mobile previews.
3. A Himalayan Yogi (YouTube & Instagram) - Visuals: Video production stills, YouTube thumbnails.

Each card should show the Challenge, Solution, and Results (measurable stats) on interaction.

Below this, add a high-impact CTA section:
Headline: Every Brand Has A Different Story.
Subtext: Explore the projects, campaigns, websites, films, and growth systems we've built.
Button: 'Explore Our Work' (Links to the new Work page).
Style: Use a large, cinematic card background for the CTA with Apple-style typography.

DO NOT modify or remove any existing sections. Only append these new sections.
name: projects/14861028082325285600/screens/1234e56ddb03478caa9d9f5ea59d6067

## Brand & Style

The design system is engineered to evoke the gravitas of a film production studio combined with the technical rigor of a high-end hardware manufacturer. It targets elite creators and enterprise partners who value discretion, quality, and narrative depth.

The aesthetic is **Cinematic Minimalism**. It leverages heavy atmospheric depth, high-contrast visual storytelling, and expansive whitespace to allow creative work to breathe. The emotional response is one of "quiet power"—a sophisticated, dark-mode environment that feels premium, curated, and indestructible.

## Colors

The palette is strictly nocturnal, designed to make imagery and cinematography the focal point.

- **Primary & Tertiary:** Deep Black (#000000) serves as the infinite canvas, while Charcoal Grey (#121212) provides structural containment for cards and navigation.
- **Accent:** Warm Gold (#D4AF37) is used sparingly for high-value interactions, status indicators, and subtle decorative elements, reminiscent of film award prestige.
- **Neutral:** Soft White (#F5F5F7) is the primary color for typography and icons, providing a crisp, legible contrast against the dark background without the harshness of pure white.

## Typography

Typography in this design system follows a highly structured, technical scale. 

- **Headlines:** Use Hanken Grotesk for its sharp, contemporary geometry. Large headlines should utilize generous letter spacing (tracking) to achieve a "luxury editorial" feel.
- **Body:** Inter provides maximum legibility for long-form descriptions and project details.
- **Technical/Labels:** Geist is used for metadata, labels, and technical specs, nodding to the precision of developer tools and camera equipment.

## Layout & Spacing

The layout philosophy is based on a **Fluid Grid with Massive Margins**. 

- **Whitespace:** Use whitespace as a structural element rather than a void. Section vertical padding should default to 160px on desktop to create a rhythmic, slow-paced browsing experience.
- **Grid:** A 12-column grid with wide 32px gutters. Content is often center-aligned or offset to create an asymmetrical, cinematic composition.
- **Mobile:** On mobile devices, margins compress to 24px, and vertical spacing reduces to 80px, maintaining the "breathable" feel without sacrificing screen real estate.

## Elevation & Depth

Depth is achieved through **Glassmorphism** and **Tonal Layering** rather than traditional drop shadows.

- **Surface Tiers:** The base layer is #000000. Elevated cards use #121212 with a 1px border of Soft White at 10% opacity.
- **Glass Effects:** Overlays, navigation bars, and floating menus use a backdrop blur of 20px and a background color of #000000 at 60% opacity.
- **Shadows:** When used for extreme emphasis, shadows should be large, ultra-diffused, and have 0% spread to mimic the natural falloff of studio lighting.

## Shapes

The shape language is **Technical and Sharp**. Elements use subtle rounding (4px to 8px) to feel modern and engineered rather than "bubbly."

- **Buttons:** Hard edges or very small radii (4px) convey precision.
- **Media:** Video thumbnails and project imagery should maintain sharp 90-degree corners to mimic a cinema screen.
- **Decorative Elements:** Use thin 1px lines (Soft White at 15% opacity) to separate content sections, reinforcing a sense of architectural structure.

## Components

### Buttons
- **Primary:** Warm Gold background, Black text. No border. High-gloss finish on hover.
- **Secondary:** Ghost style. 1px Soft White border (20% opacity), Soft White text.
- **Interaction:** On hover, primary buttons should have a subtle outer glow of the accent color.

### Cards & Overlays
- Use the glassmorphism spec: Backdrop-blur (20px), Background (#121212 at 70%), and a 1px top-light border to simulate a "beveled glass" edge.

### Inputs & Forms
- Minimalist underline style or fully enclosed dark fields (#000000). 
- Active state: The underline or border transitions to Warm Gold with a subtle 2px glow.

### Lists & Navigation
- Navigation items should use the `label-sm` spec with wide tracking.
- Hover states for list items should involve a slight horizontal shift (4px) and an increase in text opacity from 60% to 100%.

### Media Players
- Custom-skinned video controls. Seek bars should be ultra-thin (2px) using Warm Gold for the progress fill. All icons are monochrome Soft White.
